frameworks.name: 室內空氣品質門檻
frameworks.desc: 掌握 indoor air contaminants 的來源、健康影響與 A01 Air Quality 的 performance threshold，例如 PM2.5: 15 µg/m³ or lower、PM10: 50 µg/m³ or lower。

frameworks.name: 通風稀釋與新風設計
frameworks.desc: 理解 A03 Ventilation Design 與 A06 Enhanced Ventilation Design 如何用 mechanical ventilation、natural ventilation 與 ASHRAE 62.1 稀釋人員與產品產生的污染物。

frameworks.name: 污染源控制與隔離
frameworks.desc: 把 A02 Smoke-Free Environment、A04 Construction Pollution Management、A10 Combustion Minimization 與 A11 Source Separation 視為源頭移除、施工期管理與高污染空間隔離的組合。

frameworks.name: 監測過濾與微生物風險
frameworks.desc: A08 Air Quality Monitoring and Awareness、A12 Air Filtration、A13 Enhanced Supply Air 與 A14 Microbe and Mold Control 要把監測、過濾、non-recirculated or treated supply air、UVGI 與 mold control 串成運維閉環。
covers: A08 Air Quality Monitoring and Awareness

questions.stem: What are the acceptable thresholds for particulate matter for all spaces except Commercial Kitchens and Industrial spaces in unpolluted regions in Part 1: Meet Thresholds for Particulate Matter of Feature A01: Air Quality?

options.text: PM2.5: 50 ug/M or lower and PM10: 15 ug/m or lower.
PM2.5: 10 ug/M or lower and PM10: 2.5 ug/m or lower.
PM2.5: 15 ug/M or lower and PM10: 50 ug/m or lower.
PM2.5: 25 ug/M or lower and PM10: 75 ug/m or lower.

questions.source_explanation:
Part 1: Meet Thresholds for Particulate Matter of Feature A01: Air Quality has several 
different scenarios in which these thresholds should be benchmarked against that 
take into consideration what spaces are contained within the project and the 
pollution levels of the region.

questions.stem: In Feature A01: Air Quality, what is the acceptable threshold for particulate matter in commercial kitchens?
What is the verification method for Part 1: Meet Thresholds for Particulate Matter, Option 1 of Feature A01: Air Quality in all spaces except commercial kitchens and industrial spaces?
In a project with regularly occupied spaces at or below grade, where should the radon test be performed for Feature A01: Air Quality?

At an existing office building campus, signage indicating an outdoor smoking ban is posted at all building entrances. The signs indicate the ban on smoking within 7.5 m (25 ft) of all building entrances, operable windows, building air intakes and regularly occupied exterior building spaces. What signage must be added to the courtyard areas more than 7.5 m (25 ft) from building entrances to meet the requirements of Part 2: Prohibit Outdoor Smoking of Feature A02: Smoke-Free Environment?

options.text: The local municipal ordinance on smoking in exterior public areas, posted every 15 m (50 ft) along the edge of the no smoking area.
Contact information for a smoking cessation program, posted every 15 m (50 ft) along the edge of the no smoking area.
The location of designated smoking areas, posted every 30 m (100 ft) along walkways.
The hazards of smoking, posted at ground level every 30 m (100 ft) along walkways.

questions.stem: An office building's air quality test has met the minimum requirement for ozone in Part 3: Inorganic Gases of Feature A01: Air Quality. What other minimum standard is also required in Part 3 of that feature for WELL Certification?

A new office building in Seattle is in its post-occupancy performance test phase by a WELL Performance Testing Agent. What is the minimum threshold for formaldehyde in Part 2: Meet Thresholds for Organic Gases of Feature A01: Air Quality?
Which of the following features from the Air concept is a precondition for WELL Certification?

An office building has several balconies and a rooftop accessible by building occupants. According to Part 2: Prohibit Outdoor Smoking of Feature A02: Smoke-Free Environment, there must be signage at all outdoor areas in the project's boundary. What is the verification method required for this feature?
What is the maximum level of carbon monoxide for all spaces in Part 3: Meet Threshold for Inorganic Gases of Feature A05: Enhanced Air Quality?
In mechanically ventilated spaces, ventilation systems are designed to meet supply and exhaust rates set in one or more of the following verification guidelines:
questions.source_explanation: Part 1: Ensure Adequate Ventilation of Feature A03: Ventilation Design.

questions.stem: In naturally ventilated spaces with no mechanical ventilation, what requirements must be met for at least 90% of the project?
questions.source_explanation: Part 1: Ensure Adequate Ventilation of Feature A03: Ventilation Design.

questions.stem: The use of UVGI is required for which of the following features?

options.text: Feature A14: Microbe and Mold Control

questions.stem: Which of the following is NOT a part of Feature A04: Construction Pollution Management?
What are the minimum dimensions required for entryway walk-off systems?

options.text: At least the width of the doorway and 2m (6ft) long in the direction of travel.
At least 2m (6ft) wide and 3m (10ft) long in the direction of travel.
At least the width of the doorway and 3m (10ft) long in the direction of travel.
At least 2m (6ft) wide and 4m (12ft) long in the direction of travel.

questions.source_explanation: Part 1: Design Healthy Entryways of Feature A09: Pollution Infiltration Management.

questions.stem: An office space is being constructed on the 5th floor of a downtown tower. According to Feature A03: Ventilation Design, when is the ideal time to perform testing and balancing on the HVAC system in this project?
In Part 2: Meet Enhanced Thresholds for Organic Gases of Feature A05: Enhanced Air Quality, which thresholds are to be met?

options.text: Formaldehyde 9 ug/m3 or lower & Toluene 300 ug/m3 or lower.
Formaldehyde 9ug/m3 or lower & PM 2.5 12 ug/m3 or lower.
PM 2.5 12 ug/m3 or lower & Toluene 300 ug/m3 or lower.
Formaldehyde 9 ug/m3 or lower & PM10 20ug/m3 or lower.

questions.stem: For naturally ventilated spaces, it is necessary to ensure that adequate outdoor air quality & _____________ are met.
Since it is difficult to test for every potential pollutant in the indoor air of a building, what easily detectable pollutant serves as a proxy for other indoor pollutants?
questions.source_explanation: Overview of Feature A06: Enhanced Ventilation Design.

questions.stem: In Tokyo, a new mixed-use complex is being planned to replace a retired railyard. The ambient air has a high concentration of small particulates. According to Feature A12: Air Filtration, what is the project team required to install in order to protect the building occupants from exposure to the particulates?

options.text: Adequate media filters based on the annual average PM 2.5 of the area.

questions.stem: A new office building in San Diego has an average annual PM 2.5 of 21 ug/m. What type of filter is required to meet Part 1: Implement Particle Filtration of Feature A12: Air Filtration?

A new medical office building is being constructed in a suburban campus setting. The 2-story building is planned to hold 6 tenants, and the main lobby will host a 24-hour security desk and a daily parking attendant. The owner would like to pursue WELL Certification for the building. Which of the following would qualify as an adequate building entry design?

options.text: 2 sets of normally-closed doors in a series: the main building doors, and the tenant suite doors.
2 sets of normally-closed doors in a series: the main building doors and the individual office doors.
3 sets of normally-closed doors in series: the main building doors, the first floor elevator door, and the second floor elevator door.
An entryway vestibule with 2 sets of normally-closed doors.

questions.stem: In an urban high-rise building, a contractor is preparing to begin construction on a 10,000 ft2 (930m2) tenant suite. The team is preparing to use a combination of strategies during construction to mitigate the risk of construction pollution to the tenant suite. Envelope protection and filter replacement are some of the strategies that can be used. What is another strategy that can be used during construction to reduce the risk of construction pollution?
questions.source_explanation: Overview of Feature A04: Construction Pollution Management.

questions.stem: Feature A09: Pollution Infiltration Management's intent is to minimize the introduction of pollutants into indoor air through the building envelope and at building entrances. One strategy is to design healthy entryways. What else can we do to minimize pollutants in our buildings?
For which Feature in the WELL Building Standard™ (WELL™) is envelope commissioning required?
